//: Project-wide settings shared with the team. Personal overrides go in .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings for the full schema.

hooks.//: Hooks are how project rules become enforceable. The harness runs them — Claude can't bypass them via memory or context tricks. Each hook fires on a lifecycle event; see https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.

PreToolUse.//: Reject Bash commands with backslash line continuations — CLAUDE.md forbids them because they trigger approval prompts. Newlines inside a quoted string are fine.

hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.command' | grep -qE '\\\s*$' && { echo 'Reject: backslash line continuation (CLAUDE.md rule). Inline it or wrap in a quoted string.' >&2; exit 2; } || exit 0

//permissions-example: Uncomment and tune as needed. The fewer-permission-prompts skill can audit your transcripts and suggest entries.
